title: There is a presumption that the grammatical meaning of an enactment is the meaning that was intended by the legislator.

evidence.clean_text: There is a presumption that the grammatical meaning of an enactment is the meaning that was intended by the legislator. Comment The grammatical meaning is arrived at without taking into account legal considerations (see Code s 10.4). The initial presumption is in favour of the grammatical meaning, since the legislature is taken to mean what it says.
